options.info: Select a RDP Plugin, use Angrylion (if available) for best compability, GLideN64 for Performance
options.label: RDP Plugin

options.info: Select Render Viewport dimensions (4:3).
options.label: 4:3 Resolution

options.info: Select Render Viewport dimensions for wider resolutions.
options.label: Wide Resolution

options.info: Select the aspect ratio. 'adjusted' means essentially Widescreen hacks.
options.label: Aspect Ratio

options.info: Render at N times the native resolution.
options.label: Native Resolution Factor

options.info: Use the Threaded Renderer, improves performance but increases input lag.
options.label: Threaded Renderer

options.info: Select a Bilinear filtering method, 3point is the original system specific way.
options.label: Bilinear filtering mode

options.info: Hybrid integer scaling filter, this can be slow with low-end GPUs.
options.label: Hybrid Filter

options.info: Applies dithering pattern to output image.
options.label: Dithering

options.info: Dither with color quantization.
options.label: Dithering Quantization

options.info: Dithering mode for image in RDRAM.
options.label: Image Dithering Mode

options.info: Anti-Aliasing level (0 = disabled).
options.label: MSAA level

options.info: Fast Approximate Anti-Aliasing shader, moderately blur textures (0 = disabled).
options.label: FXAA

options.info: Calculate per-pixel Level Of Details to select texture mip levels and blend them with each other using LOD fraction.
options.label: LOD Emulation

options.info: Frame/depth buffer emulation. Disabling it can shorten input lag for particular games, but also break some special effects.
options.label: Framebuffer Emulation

options.info: Copy auxiliary buffers to RDRAM (fixes some Game artifacts like Paper Mario Intro).
options.label: Copy auxiliary buffers to RDRAM

options.info: Color buffer copy to RDRAM (Off will trade compatibility for Performance).
options.label: Color buffer to RDRAM

options.info: Enable color buffer copy from RDRAM.
options.label: Enable color buffer copy from RDRAM

options.info: Depth buffer copy to RDRAM (Off will trade compatibility for Performance).
options.label: Depth buffer to RDRAM

options.info: Render backgrounds mode (HLE only). One piece (fast), Stripped (precise).
options.label: Background Mode

options.info: Standard per-vertex lighting when disabled. Slightly different rendering.
options.label: Hardware per-pixel lighting

options.info: Make texrect coordinates continuous to avoid black lines between them.
options.label: Continuous texrect coords

options.info: Enables inaccurate texture coordinate calculations. This can improve performance and texture pack compatibity at the cost of accuracy.
options.label: Enable inaccurate texture coordinates

options.info: Bound texture rectangle texture coordinates to the values they take in native resolutions. It prevents garbage due to fetching out of texture bounds, but can result in hard edges.
options.label: Enable native-res boundaries for texture coordinates

options.info: Render 2D texrects in native resolution to fix misalignment between parts of 2D image (example: Mario Kart driver selection portraits).
options.label: Native res. 2D texrects

options.info: Do not use shaders to emulate N64 blending modes. Works faster on slow GPU. Can cause glitches.
options.label: Less accurate blending mode

options.info: Enable writing of fragment depth. Some mobile GPUs do not support it, thus it's optional. Leave enabled.
options.label: GPU shader depth write

options.info: Enable N64 depth compare instead of OpenGL standard one. Experimental, Fast mode will have more glitches.
options.label: N64 Depth Compare

options.info: Use persistent storage for compiled shaders.
options.label: Cache GPU Shaders

options.info: Save texture cache to hard disk.
options.label: Cache Textures

options.info: Crop black borders from the overscan region around the screen.
options.label: Overscan

options.info: Overscan Top Offset.
options.label: Overscan Offset (Top)

options.info: Overscan Left Offset.
options.label: Overscan Offset (Left)

options.info: Overscan Right Offset.
options.label: Overscan Offset (Right)

options.info: Overscan Bottom Offset.
options.label: Overscan Offset (Bottom)

options.info: Limit High-Res textures size in VRAM (in MB, 0 = no limit).
options.label: Max High-Res VRAM Limit

options.info: Set Max texture cache size (in elements). Reduce it if you experience black textures leading to a crash.
options.label: Max texture cache size

options.info: Select Texture Filtering mode.
options.label: Texture filter

options.info: Various Texture Filters ('As-Is' will just cache).
options.label: Texture Enhancement

options.info: Ignore filtering for Background Textures.
options.label: Don't filter background textures

options.info: Enable High-Res Texture packs if available.
options.label: Use High-Res textures

options.info: Compress created texture caches.
options.label: Use High-Res Texture Cache Compression

options.info: This should be enabled unless it's a old RICE Texture pack.
options.label: Use High-Res Full Alpha Channel

options.info: Use in addition to Texture cache, will use lazy loading and trade memory consumption against loading speeds.
options.label: Use enhanced Texture Storage

options.info: Use an alternative method for High-Res paletted textures CRC calculations.
options.label: Use alternative method for High-Res Checksums

options.info: Use in addition to High-Res textures, will use lazy loading and trade memory consumption against loading speeds.
options.label: Use enhanced Hi-Res Storage

options.info: Specifies INI Settings behaviour. This should really only contain essential options. Changing this can and will break ROM's, if the correct options aren't set manually. Some options may only be set via INI (fbInfoDisabled).
options.label: INI Behaviour

options.info: Select VI Overlay filtering. 'Filtered' is the original system rendering.
options.label: VI Overlay

options.info: Select Sync level (trades accuracy for performance).
options.label: Thread sync level

options.info: Default 'all threads' is prefered to have it match your Physical CPU Core count. '1' should behave as the original angrylion, possibly fixing some bugs.
options.label: Multi-threading

options.info: Hide overscan borders.
options.label: Hide overscan

options.info: Select the R4300 CPU Backend, use Interpreter for best compability
options.label: CPU Core

options.info: Select a RSP Plugin, use HLE for best performance, ParaLLEl for best LLE Performance and CXD4 as LLE fallback
options.label: RSP Plugin

options.info: Enable Frame duplication to improve smoothing on low-end. Different from frameskip.
options.label: Frame Duplication

options.info: Fullspeed will enforce Count per Op 1 and FBEmu settings, this will break some games!
options.label: Framerate

options.info: Select a VI Refresh clock, Auto does not impact behaviour, other values override CountPerScanline.
options.label: VI Refresh (Overclock)

options.info: Force disable Expansion Pak (might improve performance for some games while reducing emulation accuracy, will break games that require it).
options.label: Disable Expansion Pak

options.info: (HACK) Ignore emulated TLB Exceptions, this might fix some broken romhacks. This option might be removed in the future.
options.label: Ignore emulated TLB Exceptions

values.label: Ignore TLB Exceptions if not using TLB

options.info: Count per Op is used to approximate the Counter reg, 0 will use the embedded Database (or default to 2). Changing this will break stuff!
options.label: Count Per Op

options.info: Denominator for Count per Op (allowing sub-1 Count per Op in practice). Changing this can break stuff!
options.label: Count Per Op Divider (Overclock)

options.info: Size of the non responsive area around an analog stick.
options.label: Analog Deadzone (percent)

options.info: Adjust how far the stick needs to be moved to reach its max value.
options.label: Analog Sensitivity (percent)

options.info: Select Right C Button mapping.
options.label: Right C Button

options.info: Select Left C Button mapping.
options.label: Left C Button

options.info: Select Down C Button mapping.
options.label: Down C Button

options.info: Select Up C Button mapping.
options.label: Up C Button

options.info: Use an alternate control scheme, useful for some 3rdparty controllers.
options.label: Independent C-button Controls

options.info: Select Player 1 Controller Pak.
options.label: Player 1 Pak

options.info: Select Player 2 Controller Pak.
options.label: Player 2 Pak

options.info: Select Player 3 Controller Pak.
options.label: Player 3 Pak

options.info: Select Player 4 Controller Pak.
options.label: Player 4 Pak
